name: Ip Incredipawn Jewelry & Firearms
description_short: IncrediPawn is a pawn shop offering loans up to $1,800 against collateral items including jewelry, firearms, electronics, and tools at two Texas locations in Fort Worth and Arlington.
description_long:
IncrediPawn operates as a traditional pawn shop with multiple years of service in the Fort Worth and Arlington, Texas markets. The company positions itself as offering a dual-purpose retail experience—functioning both as a lender and a reseller of secondhand goods. Their core business model centers on providing quick cash loans secured by personal items of value, with maximum loan amounts up to $1,800. The company emphasizes competitive pricing and accessibility, positioning themselves as a solution for customers needing immediate liquidity or seeking specific inventory items at discounted prices.

The company explicitly offers collateral-based loans and maintains an inventory of merchandise categories including engagement rings, electronics, firearms, tools, and jewelry. Customers can either pledge items as collateral for short-term loans or purchase from IncrediPawn's existing inventory. The website indicates loans are available for "just about anything of value," suggesting flexibility in accepted collateral types. The company operates two physical locations with extended hours (9 AM–7 PM weekdays in Fort Worth; 9 AM–6 PM weekdays in Arlington) to serve customer convenience.

IncrediPawn distinguishes itself through its explicit firearms inventory category—a service not all pawn shops prominently advertise—and its two-location physical presence in the Dallas-Fort Worth metropolitan area. The company emphasizes customer education, noting they are "happy to explain how our system works to new customers." Their brand messaging focuses on fair valuation ("Top Dollar Paid") and the breadth of accepted collateral types.

As a pawn shop, IncrediPawn caters to consumers in immediate financial need with tangible assets to pledge. The primary caveat is that pawn loans involve surrendering ownership of items, interest and fees apply (not disclosed on the website), and loan terms are typically short-term. This service is appropriate for temporary liquidity needs but not for long-term borrowing or consumers without collateral. The lack of transparent fee/APR disclosure on the website is a notable limitation for consumer evaluation.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Immediate cash available—loans can be obtained same-day with collateral appraisal
Accepts wide range of collateral including jewelry, firearms, electronics, and tools
Loans up to $1,800, higher than typical payday loan limits
Two convenient locations in Fort Worth and Arlington with extended operating hours
No credit check required—collateral-based lending accessible to consumers with poor credit
Dual-purpose retail model allows customers to purchase discounted secondhand items or pledge for loans
Established business with "many years" of market presence suggesting reliability
cons: No transparent disclosure of interest rates, fees, or loan terms on website
Requires surrendering ownership of personal items as collateral during loan period
Limited to two locations in Texas; not accessible to customers outside Fort Worth/Arlington area
Short-term lending structure unsuitable for consumers needing long-term financial solutions
Firearms inventory may present regulatory complexity and limits eligibility in some jurisdictions
best_for: Consumers with immediate cash needs and valuable collateral (jewelry, electronics, tools) they can temporarily surrender
Individuals with poor credit history unable to qualify for traditional personal loans
People seeking to purchase discounted secondhand merchandise including firearms or jewelry
Borrowers in the Fort Worth/Arlington area needing same-day liquidity for short-term financial gaps
diagnosis: IncrediPawn is best for consumers in the Fort Worth/Arlington area with valuable personal property who need immediate cash and can accept short-term collateral-based lending. The primary caveat is that pawn loans require surrendering items temporarily, APR/fee structures are not disclosed online, and this product is unsuitable for long-term borrowing needs—consumers should compare terms against payday alternatives or personal loans before pledging collateral.
services: New customer consultations and system explanations
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Pawn shop offering loans up to $1,800 against jewelry, firearms, electronics, and tools at two Texas locations in Fort Worth and Arlington.
